title: HOP_BentoBoxDS_Core_Vocabulary_Ledger
description: Foundational dictionary defining the protocol machinery, spatial-temporal engines, and cryptographic infrastructure used to render the runtime interface dynamically from the local library.

lifestrap.definition: The sovereign root container of identity, personal intent, and historical ledger continuity held locally by a peer.

story.definition: The natural language first entry point or zero-draft mode where a peer states their qualitative health or longevity inquiries.

interplay.definition: The active conversational and computational translation phase transforming a raw story text into a structural model of tracking variables.

emulation.definition: The high-fidelity execution layer where local biological streams, environmental data, and WASM logic run code loops to model real-world health outcomes.

lens.definition: The multi-column tabular interface mapping words into structured spaces of capacity, context, and attunement vectors.

capacity.definition: The column domain detailing the active biological or structural thresholds a peer is capable of performing or measuring.

context.definition: The combined internal and external environmental factors encompassing the physical body, domestic buildings, and bioregional earth coordinates.

attunement.definition: The strategic regulatory vector chosen by a peer, dividing health activities into prevention, repair, or deep rejuvenation targets.

besearch.definition: Collaborative, decentralized network research cycles structured as open-ended shared data loops that peers can seamlessly join or leave.

strand.definition: A single, coherent path of linked tokens drawn from across the lens columns to initiate an isolated instance of a network experiment.

lensglue.definition: The relational contract that binds variable user-defined concepts to immutable physical and biological primitives within the library.

conduction.definition: The core paradigm of interdependent, autonomous data flow across the network, deliberately avoiding isolationist sovereignty.

peer.definition: An equal, sovereign participant node within the decentralized system, operating on local-first protocols with no upstream authority.

boreal.definition: The overarching vision of distributed, seasonal community networks interacting directly with regional ecology and sovereign informational portals.

bentobox.definition: The local-first hardware and data-store enclosure optimized for secure, isolated processing of personal records.

oracle.definition: A local or federated computation routine that verifies physical inputs and maps them into the decentralized ledger without trusted third parties.

soil.definition: The local Hyperbee storage layer where binary indices, raw records, and cryptographic relationships are firmly rooted.

nil.definition: A blank-slate primitive representing a completely unassigned or newly initiated concept before its structural parameters are formalised.

graft.definition: A dynamic, biological-style contract connection that joins disparate machine-to-machine schema patterns without generating brittle API dependencies.

heli.definition: The core timekeeping module tracking real solar mechanics and planetary coordinate vectors rather than arbitrary civil time structures.

orrery.definition: The Geometric Temporal Engine tracking and aligning local data routines according to astronomical, solar, and bioregional rhythms.

coherence.definition: The metric measuring how closely a peer's data entries align structurally with the verification models set by the public schema.

resonance.definition: The state achieved when multiple distinct local data loops align harmoniously in frequency, duration, and baseline purpose.

resonagent.definition: Local autonomous entities that evaluate, schedule, and orchestrate underlying data pipelines inside the local peer node.

stitch.definition: A deterministic cryptographic link binding a private lifestrap hash to an entry point in the global content-addressable dictionary.

energy-scheduling.definition: An internal orchestration model prioritizing script processing and sensor tracking according to solar energy availability and biological cycles.

buffer-geometry.definition: The allocation of flat, deterministic memory spaces designed for raw performance rendering and local WebGPU streaming.

cue.definition: A distinct semantic signpost or name mapping a human-readable term to an active computational process inside the network.

datatype.definition: The structural, invariant schema validating primitive data formats like raw heart rate numbers, body mass metrics, or coordinate sets.

ledger.definition: An append-only, tamper-evident local sequence logging all validation transitions and execution milestones securely over time.

harvester.definition: A dedicated ingestion worker that captures native hardware telemetry directly from wearable devices, scales, and ambient instruments.

swarm.definition: The ad-hoc clustering of peer nodes running the same distributed experiment to coordinate data sharing without cloud intermediaries.

holepunch.definition: The networking utility layer responsible for breaking through NAT boundaries to establish direct, hyper-local peer connections.

hyperbee.definition: A decentralized B-tree indexing database built over append-only feeds, acting as the fundamental structure for fast local sorting.

witness.definition: A peer node acting as an independent validating observer to confirm the structural integrity of a transaction block or telemetry file.

bioregion.definition: The physical geography boundary governing local energy allocation, climate inputs, and shared environmental criteria.

pulse.definition: A standardized biological metric capturing regular, rhythmic indicators like arterial pressure wave events or muscle firing paces.

prevention.definition: A protective vector dedicated to monitoring and dampening disruptive biological shifts before they mutate baseline thresholds.

repair.definition: An active restorative vector deploying localized interventions to re-stabilize broken structural systems or damaged telemetry loops.

rejuvenate.definition: A long-term cellular vector optimizing metabolic performance to slowly extend baseline biological vitality thresholds far into old age.
